title: Mixology Certification

jokes.text: We only sang the last two words. What happened to the 'happy birthday' part?
jokes.setup: Study group sings abbreviated birthday song
jokes.punchline: Annie notices the absurdity of singing only 'to you'
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that sets up the religious accommodation joke. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: Hello during a random dessert, the month and day of which coincide numerically with your expulsion from a uterus
jokes.setup: Need to make birthday cake compliant with Jehovah's Witness beliefs
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical and absurd rewording of 'happy birthday'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist wordplay that's both ridiculous and technically accurate. Highly quotable Community-style humor.

jokes.text: Last week we had fondue and played Boggle because Shirley's niece took her first bath
jokes.setup: Annie complaining about group avoiding studying
jokes.punchline: Absurd example of trivial celebration excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Annie's controlling nature, with absurd specificity that makes it funnier.

jokes.text: With bubbles. Thank you, it's a milestone.
jokes.setup: Defense of celebrating niece's first bath
jokes.punchline: Sincere treatment of trivial event as milestone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley character beat - sincere about everything, including absurd things.

jokes.text: Pierce, you don't remember the huge party we threw?
jokes.setup: Pierce complains no one remembered his birthday
jokes.punchline: Reveal that they did throw him a party he forgot
jokes.explanation: Sets up Pierce's memory issues while creating situational irony.

jokes.text: Huge party, you were like, "The best party of my life." - Nutritious. - Got you!
jokes.setup: Trying to help Pierce remember his birthday party
jokes.punchline: Pierce thinking 'nutritious' is the right response, then claiming he was testing them
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Pierce's confusion and face-saving attempts

jokes.text: Yeah, you still owe me for the keg deposit.
jokes.setup: Pierce claiming to remember his birthday party
jokes.punchline: Specific detail that proves the party happened
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that adds practical consequence to the forgotten party.

jokes.text: I got a cousin in Detroit, they're not crazy about it.
jokes.setup: Movie 'Kickpuncher: Detroit' is sold out everywhere
jokes.punchline: Detroit residents aren't enthusiastic about their city's portrayal
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor about civic pride and exploitation films.

jokes.text: I broke my legs, not my gender.
jokes.setup: Someone offers to help Jeff with cake
jokes.punchline: Jeff's overly defensive response to normal courtesy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff line - witty, defensive masculinity expressed cleverly. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Also born that day, Tyra Banks, Marisa Tomei and French cinematographer Claude Renoir
jokes.setup: Abed giving birthday facts about Troy's birthday
jokes.punchline: Increasingly esoteric reference to film industry figure
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed - mixing pop culture with film knowledge. Character-perfect but not exceptional craft.

jokes.text: Which would make me 20 because everyone is 10 for two years. Because fifth grade is really hard for everyone.
jokes.setup: Math confusion about Troy's age
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic about universal grade repetition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - childlike reasoning delivered with complete confidence. Absurdist and character-driven.

jokes.text: Mom, how many lies have I been living?
jokes.setup: Troy realizing he's been wrong about his age
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction to simple math error
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Troy's tendency toward existential drama over minor issues.

jokes.text: Think of it as Troy taking his first bath, only the bubbles are his manhood.
jokes.setup: Calling back to Shirley's niece's first bath celebration
jokes.punchline: Absurd metaphor connecting bathing to masculine maturation
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that creates absurd parallel between innocent bath and adult milestone.

jokes.text: Yes. I wanna bathe in manhood.
jokes.setup: Jeff's bathing metaphor
jokes.punchline: Troy enthusiastically embracing the ridiculous metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy response - taking metaphor literally and enthusiastically.

jokes.text: Douche Street.
jokes.setup: Jeff mentions L Street
jokes.punchline: Annie's immediate dismissive nickname
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective nickname showing Annie's judgmental side.

jokes.text: No, but I also haven't been to Beirut. But I'm sure I will go one day because I hear they have lots of important cultural...
jokes.setup: Jeff asking if Annie has been to L Street
jokes.punchline: Annie's overly dramatic comparison that trails off awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annie getting flustered and defensive.

jokes.text: Not hipster. Hipsters haven't discovered it yet, it's underground.
jokes.setup: Jeff calling Red Door hipster
jokes.punchline: Britta's completely hipster response denying hipsterness
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta self-contradiction - being hipster while denying hipsterness.

jokes.text: They serve virgin mudslides. Those are milk shakes.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to go to Peg Leg Pablo's for virgin mudslides
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt deflation of the pretentious drink name
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline with great economy. Jeff cutting through pretension.

jokes.text: Real, it just won't be yours.
jokes.setup: Annie asking about fake ID
jokes.punchline: Clarification that reveals identity theft implication
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with dark comedy undertone about identity fraud.

jokes.text: We're not going to Red Poet's Society.
jokes.setup: Britta suggesting the Red Door
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dismissive renaming combining pretension with movie reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Jeff's disdain for perceived pretension.

jokes.text: I don't need people helping me do normal things and doting over me.
jokes.setup: Jeff in wheelchair being offered help
jokes.punchline: Jeff's defensive independence while clearly needing assistance
jokes.explanation: Character-driven irony about Jeff's pride conflicting with reality.

jokes.text: So she's a drifter. A floater. An urchin.
jokes.setup: Learning about people selling IDs
jokes.punchline: Annie's increasingly judgmental descriptors
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Annie's tendency to judge and categorize people.

jokes.text: That's Cajun.
jokes.setup: Annie doing terrible Texas accent
jokes.punchline: Britta's matter-of-fact correction about wrong regional accent
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective correction with good economy.

jokes.text: Who probably has really bad credit and an unfinished mermaid tattoo.
jokes.setup: Annie imagining Caroline Decker's life
jokes.punchline: Specific judgmental assumptions about the ID's owner
jokes.explanation: Specific and character-driven assumptions that reveal Annie's prejudices humorously.

jokes.text: It's either got a gross name or an ironically fancy one, possibly both.
jokes.setup: Describing dive bars in general
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cynical but accurate assessment of bar naming conventions
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about bar culture with good structure.

jokes.text: And now I guess I am being clever.
jokes.setup: Jeff making a play on 'The Ballroom' name
jokes.punchline: Jeff acknowledging his own wordplay
jokes.explanation: Meta moment that's mildly amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Troy, you're ruining Fuddruckers for everyone.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining about plastic menus and spills
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd consequence at chain restaurant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's specific childhood experiences.

jokes.text: Sadly, it's the final chapter, but it's also the longest, and if you play it right, the best.
jokes.setup: Pierce talking about Troy entering adulthood
jokes.punchline: Pierce's morbid perspective on life chapters
jokes.explanation: Characteristically dark Pierce wisdom with underlying morbidity.

jokes.text: You don't know me. Okay? Yeah. You don't know me.
jokes.setup: Bartender recognizing Shirley at the bar
jokes.punchline: Shirley's awkward denial of her past bar patronage
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment revealing Shirley's hidden past and current embarrassment.

jokes.text: I don't say this often, Troy, but dream a little smaller.
jokes.setup: Troy wondering if he could make it to the wall of regulars
jokes.punchline: Pierce's backhanded advice to lower expectations
jokes.explanation: Excellent inversion of typical encouragement with perfect Pierce cynicism.

jokes.text: Corpus Christi, Texas. 78418.
jokes.setup: Bartender asking about Annie's accent origin
jokes.punchline: Annie over-preparing with unnecessary zip code detail
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annie's over-preparation tendency.

jokes.text: Just drifting, floating. Spitting in the wind. General waywardness.
jokes.setup: Bartender asking what Caroline is doing in town
jokes.punchline: Annie's overly romantic description of aimless lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Annie romanticizing the lifestyle she previously judged.

jokes.text: Don't order beer. You've had beer. What should I get? What do you think you might like? It's your world now. Follow your heart.
jokes.setup: Trying to help Troy pick his first legal drink
jokes.punchline: Escalating from practical to overly philosophical advice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how choosing a drink becomes existential

jokes.text: Don't order that. That's an awful drink. A high-school drink for girls.
jokes.setup: Troy wants seven and seven to honor dead uncle
jokes.punchline: Brutally dismissing the sentimental choice
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment where sentiment gets crushed by snobbery

jokes.text: You score any higher on this, the Rylans are gonna recruit you to fight the Ko-Dan Empire.
jokes.setup: Abed playing arcade game well
jokes.punchline: Obscure reference to The Last Starfighter plot
jokes.explanation: Niche reference humor that works for the target audience but limited appeal.

jokes.text: You're a fan of the sci-fi original series Farscape. Can I buy you a drink?
jokes.setup: Robert recognizing Abed's sci-fi knowledge
jokes.punchline: Direct proposition based on shared nerdy interest
jokes.explanation: Sets up the coming gay proposition subplot with nerdy connection.

jokes.text: Back in Corpus Christi, they call me Capricious Caroline.
jokes.setup: Annie describing her fake persona's reputation
jokes.punchline: Annie using a vocabulary word she doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Good irony as studious Annie creates persona using words she doesn't know.

jokes.text: It probably means I'm too busy living life to be learning $5 words.
jokes.setup: Guy asking what capricious means
jokes.punchline: Annie's anti-intellectual response while clearly knowing expensive vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony as intellectual Annie pretends to be anti-intellectual while using precise vocabulary.

jokes.text: The too-cool-to-care-tini?
jokes.setup: Jeff criticizing Britta's drink order
jokes.punchline: Britta's defensive cocktail name pun
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that feels forced, typical of Britta's defensive responses.

jokes.text: Hey, Ms. Vodka-Neat-Four-Olives. What's that called? The too-cool-to-care-tini?
jokes.setup: Mocking pretentious drink order
jokes.punchline: Making up drink name ending in '-tini' to mock it
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay mocking pretentious drink culture

jokes.text: I gotta go see a woman about the female equivalent of a horse.
jokes.setup: Annie excusing herself from the bar
jokes.punchline: Overwrought euphemism for using the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Clever play on common euphemism showing Annie's overthinking tendency.

jokes.text: That woman is a hurricane. Yeah. Hurricanes are bad, Troy.
jokes.setup: Pierce calling Annie (as Caroline) a hurricane
jokes.punchline: Troy's literal interpretation missing the metaphorical meaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Troy's literal thinking and Pierce trying to guide him.

jokes.text: That doesn't make sense. Wormholes and teleportation are different.
jokes.setup: Robert's clumsy sci-fi pickup line about wormholes
jokes.punchline: Abed's literal correction of bad science
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - completely missing social cues while being technically accurate.

jokes.text: Plans just fall off me like chicken crap off an armadillo.
jokes.setup: Annie as Caroline talking about not planning
jokes.punchline: Overly colorful Southern metaphor
jokes.explanation: Amusing folksy metaphor that shows Annie overcommitting to the Southern persona.

jokes.text: Abed, would you like to have gay sex with me?
jokes.setup: Long buildup of Robert hitting on Abed through sci-fi talk
jokes.punchline: Blunt direct proposition after subtle approach fails
jokes.explanation: Shocking directness after buildup creates strong cringe comedy moment.

jokes.text: I actually did pick up on it after a while. And? I really like talking about Farscape.
jokes.setup: Robert asking why Abed didn't pick up on being hit on
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact prioritizing of sci-fi discussion over social interaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed character moment - socially aware but prioritizing interests over social conventions. Excellent craft and character integration.

jokes.text: We found it in the ladies' room.
jokes.setup: Shirley praying for souls in photographs
jokes.punchline: Reveal that Shirley's photo was found in inappropriate location
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Shirley's embarrassment and backstory revelation.

jokes.text: You look like a zombie.
jokes.setup: Describing Shirley's photo appearance
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of unflattering photo
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with good economy but limited craft.

jokes.text: You're welcome, jackass.
jokes.setup: Pierce thanking Shirley for helping him
jokes.punchline: Shirley's polite response followed by insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Shirley's internal conflict between politeness and anger.

jokes.text: It's the same bar?
jokes.setup: Long argument about L Street vs Red Door
jokes.punchline: Troy's realization they've been arguing about the same place
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to running gag with perfect structure and timing. Major story revelation.

jokes.text: You two have been saying one bar's lame and the other one is awesome all night, and it's the same bar?
jokes.setup: Jeff and Britta's long argument about different bars
jokes.punchline: Troy's realization they've been arguing about the same place
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to running gag, Troy's dawning realization is perfectly timed and executed

jokes.text: I just found out that you guys are just as dumb as me. Duh-doy.
jokes.setup: Troy's realization about the bar situation
jokes.punchline: Troy's matter-of-fact assessment and made-up word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment of surprising wisdom delivered in characteristically childlike way.

jokes.text: Alcohol makes people sad. It's like the Lifetime movies of beverages.
jokes.setup: Troy commenting on the night's events
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly insightful metaphor about alcohol's effects
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected wisdom from Troy with perfect metaphor. Highly quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: You expect everybody to be better than who they are and you expect yourself to be better than everyone.
jokes.setup: Troy analyzing Annie's character
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly accurate psychological insight
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Troy's hidden emotional intelligence and insight.

jokes.text: You like puzzles, little monsters on your pencil and some guy named Mark Ruffalo.
jokes.setup: Troy describing what he knows about Annie
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and accurate observations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's observational skills despite seeming oblivious

jokes.text: They were making out. Abed. Why would you do it in front of me? I'm not a coat rack.
jokes.setup: Jeff and Britta's romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact reporting and complaint about being ignored
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment treating awkward situation with documentary objectivity.

jokes.text: Hurry. I think it's a girl. Left side or right side? Left side. Got it. Tell me when I'm close.
jokes.setup: Someone needs help with something urgent
jokes.punchline: Awkward coordination suggesting bathroom emergency
jokes.explanation: End tag with mild physical comedy, relies on bathroom humor

tmdb_overview: Troy's friends help him celebrate his 21st birthday.
insight: Bar crawl episode leans hard on character dynamics over setup-punchline structure.
